data.content:
I can see the problem. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file, but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. SDK-style implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before targets run), so the file doesn't exist yet to be discovered.

The fix: add the generated file to `Compile` and `FileWrites` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs are evaluated at evaluation time, before any targets run, so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after the file is created. Also, registering it with `<FileWrites>` ensures the Clean target removes it.

This is the classic "including-generated-files" pattern.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The target generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` at build time, but SDK-style implicit globs (`**/*.cs`) are evaluated during *project evaluation* — before any targets run. Since the file doesn't exist yet at evaluation time, it's never included in compilation.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>`, which explicitly adds the generated file to the compiler inputs at execution time and registers it for cleanup by `dotnet clean`.
